MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorney David Wasserman for Defendant's Meridian Management Group, A California corporation the moving party.

Plaintiff Scott Dykes the opposing party in Pro Per.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of DEFENDANTs RUSSELL B. FLYNN INVESTMENT COMPANY L.L.C., and RUSSELL B. FLYNN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

GRANTED. Defendants shifted their burden. Plaintiff failed to present competent admissible evidence to show that a triable issue of fact exists. Plaintiff's request to continue under CCP 437c is denied. This request is not supported by affidavits. Additionally, discovery information from Plaintiff's own medical provider and from the Assessor's Office has been available to Plaintiff since this case was filed on February 10, 2016 and Plaintiff was served with the Motion for Summary Judgment on May 1, 2018, i.e. 2.5 months before opposition was due. Order and Judgment signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Recorded. (501)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint For Personal Injury And Property Damage
Off Calendar Jan-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SCOTT DYKES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR PERSONAL INJURY AND PROPERTY DAMAGE

Appearances: D. Mark Jackson and T. Connor O'Carroll of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum (415.397.9006) for defendant Meridian Management Group; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court corrects its ruling as follows: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Court notes that Plaintiff again failed to comply with CRC 3.1324, specifically, 3.1324(a)(2)-(3), (b)(4). The Court also notes that per Notice Motion and Motion Plaintiff solely relies on CCP 473 and 576 and not on CCP 474 for the amendment. Motion is DENIED as to the proposed amendment to include Meridian Management Group, Inc., Lucerne Apartments, LP (and its partners/members/managers). Plaintiff failed to establish that involvement of these entities with the ownership/management of the premises or the identity of these entities were not known to Plaintiff prior to the expiration of the statute of limitations and/or at the time of the filing of the complaint. Plaintiff failed to show that the inclusion of these entities without reliance on "Doe" amendment will not be barred by the statute of limitations. The motion is GRANTED as to the inclusion of allegations regarding lead contamination. The Court is not making any rulings as to the application of the statute of limitations/relation back as to this allegation. Order signed. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
